events.title: Phone Bill Shock
events.prompt: Your phone bill came in higher than expected because you went over your data limit. You need to decide how to handle it.

choices.label: Pay the full bill now and downgrade your plan
choices.learningNote: Paying in full avoids compounding interest. Downgrading your plan also reduces future bills — a double win.

choices.label: Pay the minimum and deal with the rest later
choices.learningNote: Minimum payments keep you in good standing but the remaining balance accrues interest, costing more over time.

events.explanation: Paying bills in full avoids interest charges. If a bill is too high, look for ways to reduce future costs like switching plans.

events.title: Friend Group Plans
events.prompt: Your friends want to go to an escape room this weekend. It costs $25 per person, but you only budgeted $15 for fun this month.

choices.label: Go anyway and overspend your fun budget
choices.learningNote: Overspending your 'fun' category means borrowing from savings or essentials — a slippery slope.

choices.label: Suggest a cheaper activity instead
choices.learningNote: Proposing alternatives keeps you social AND on budget. Negotiation is a key financial life skill.

choices.label: Skip this time and save for next month
choices.learningNote: Sometimes saying no is the smartest financial move. You can plan ahead for next month's outing.

events.explanation: Peer pressure can wreck a budget. Suggesting alternatives shows leadership and keeps your finances healthy.

events.title: First Paycheck!
events.prompt: You got your first paycheck from your part-time job: $120! Time to make a plan for it.

choices.label: 50-30-20 split: needs, wants, savings
Save it all
Treat yourself — you earned it!

events.explanation: The 50-30-20 rule is a popular budgeting method: 50% needs, 30% wants, 20% savings. It helps balance enjoying money AND growing it.

events.title: Subscription Overload
events.prompt: You just realized you're paying for 4 different streaming services. You only really watch 2 of them.

choices.label: Cancel the two you don't use
Keep them all — you might watch them later

events.explanation: Subscription creep is when small recurring charges add up without you noticing. Auditing subscriptions regularly saves real money.

events.title: Flat Tire!
events.prompt: Your bike got a flat tire on the way to work. A repair costs $15, or you can buy a patch kit for $5 and fix it yourself.

choices.label: Pay $15 for the shop to fix it
Buy the $5 patch kit and learn to fix it

events.explanation: Learning basic repairs saves money long term. The patch kit can be reused, making future fixes nearly free!

events.title: Flash Sale Alert!
events.prompt: Your favorite store is having a 50% off sale! The jacket you wanted is $40 instead of $80. But it wasn't in your budget.

choices.label: Buy it — half price is too good to miss!
Wait 24 hours and decide tomorrow
Move $40 from next month's wants budget

events.explanation: A sale only saves money if you were going to buy it anyway. The 24-hour rule helps avoid impulse purchases.

events.title: Side Hustle Opportunity
events.prompt: A neighbor asks if you can walk their dog every afternoon for $8/day. It would take about an hour after school.

choices.label: Accept — steady extra income!
Decline — need time for homework

events.explanation: Side income is great, but time is also valuable. Balance earning with your other responsibilities like school.

events.title: Lost Textbook Fee
events.prompt: The school says you lost a textbook and owe a $30 replacement fee. You don't remember losing it, but the fee is due Friday.

choices.label: Pay from your emergency fund
Search everywhere for the book first
Ask to set up a payment plan

events.explanation: Emergency funds exist for surprise expenses. If you don't have one, negotiating a payment plan is the next best option.
